c_signature: int mpfr_set_sj (mpfr_ptr x, intmax_t j, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_set_sj: set the result to j, correctly rounded to prec bits per rnd, where j is a signed C intmax_t (int64 on the platforms we care about). The C body (mpfr/src/set_sj.c L27-L31) is a one-line delegate: mpfr_set_sj(x, j, rnd) = mpfr_set_sj_2exp(x, j, 0, rnd).

The TS port takes j: bigint (per ADR 0003 mpz-as-bigint convention, here constrained to signed int64 domain [-2^63, 2^63-1]) and delegates to the already-shipped src/ops/set_sj_2exp.ts with e = 0n.

Tag minimums per Rule 7: happy 20, edge 30, adversarial 12, fuzz 50, mined 5. Class 'conversion' (200ms budget); slot matches mpfr_set_sj_2exp which has identical class.
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. j parameter: C intmax_t (int64) -> TS bigint, constrained to [-(2^63), 2^63 - 1] -- same constraint as set_sj_2exp. Throws MPFRError('EDOMAIN') if j is outside int64 range.

  2. Return: C mutates rop in place + returns int ternary; TS returns {value, ternary} Result.

  3. emax/emin: C clamps result against the active __gmpfr_emax / __gmpfr_emin. TS has no exponent range surface (per src/core.ts schema). For mpfr_set_sj with e=0 implicit, the resulting exponent is bounded by 63 (since |j| < 2^63) -- far inside any reasonable MPFR exponent range; the divergence is invisible.
refs: mpfr/src/set_sj.c L27-L31 -- the C reference body (one-line delegate).
src/ops/set_sj_2exp.ts -- the underlying delegate; this port wraps it with e = 0n.
eval/functions/mpfr_set_sj_2exp/golden_driver.c -- structural template; this driver mirrors the same xorshift PRNG seeding pattern and tag distribution, with e fixed at 0.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' -- ternary is sign of (rounded - exact).
